messages.content:
# Prompt: spec-draft.system

version: 0.1.0
target artifact: AIL-Spec Canonical

## Purpose

Convert checked AIL-Requirements into canonical structured English accepted by
the parser.

## Input Schema

```json
{
  "requirements": "",
  "profile": "",
  "package_manifest": "",
  "required_features": []
}
```

## Output Schema

Use the common prompt envelope with `artifact_kind` set to
`AIL-Spec Canonical`.

## Forbidden Behavior

- Do not use friendly paraphrase where canonical grammar is required.
- Do not add default failures, approvals, traces, or external calls without
  provenance.
- Do not suppress unresolved questions.

## Provenance And Handoff

Each canonical section includes provenance back to requirements. The generated
spec is handed to the parser and checker.

## Valid Example

```text
Action: Close ticket.

When a support agent closes a ticket:

- the system requires the ticket to exist
- the system changes the ticket status to Closed
- the system records a trace event named TicketClosed
```

## Invalid Example

```text
The app should probably close tickets normally.
```

Reason: not canonical, missing actor, writes, and trace.

## Round-Trip Expectation

AIL-Spec Canonical renders to AIL-Core and back without semantic hash drift.

TASK:
Return only complete canonical AIL-Spec text for the AIL standard collections package.

Use these exact section forms:
- Package: ail.std.collections.
- Type: Option<T>.
- Option has variants:
- Type: Result<T,E>.
- Result has variants:
- Type: List<T>.
- List has variants:
- Type: Map<K,V>.
- Map has variants:
- Type: Set<T>.
- Set has variants:
- Function: Option.map.
- The function needs:
- The function produces:
- When Option.map runs:

Preserve generic type names exactly, including Option<T>, Result<T,E>, List<T>,
Map<K,V>, Set<T>, Option<U>, Some(value: T), Success(value: T), and
Failure(error: E). Preserve the trace event name OptionMapEvaluated.

Do not use markdown fences, commentary, or alternative headings.

INPUT JSON:
{
  "package_manifest": "# AIL Package\n\nname: ail.std.collections\nversion: 0.2.0\nprofile: Application\nentry: spec.ail-spec.md\nfeatures: stdlib, types, functions, collection-transform, traces\nimports: ../ail_std_core.ail compatible ^0.2 as Core\nconformance: v0.2\nschema-version: ail-core.schema.v0\nsafety-level: standard\ntarget-support:\n  ail-core.schema.v0: supported\n",
  "profile": "Application",
  "required_features": [
    "stdlib",
    "types",
    "functions",
    "collection-transform",
    "traces"
  ],
  "requirements": "AIL-Requirements:\n- The package ail.std.collections defines reusable collection and result types.\n- The package defines Type Option<T> with variants Some(value: T) and None.\n- The package defines Type Result<T,E> with variants Success(value: T) and Failure(error: E).\n- The package defines Type List<T> with variants Empty and Items(values: T).\n- The package defines Type Map<K,V> with variants Empty and Entries(key: K, value: V).\n- The package defines Type Set<T> with variants Empty and Members(value: T).\n- The package defines Function Option.map.\n- Option.map needs option: Option<T> and mapper: Text.\n- Option.map produces result: Option<U>.\n- When Option.map runs, if the option is Some(value), the function calls mapper with value, returns Some(mapped value), and records a trace event named OptionMapEvaluated.\n- When Option.map runs, if the option is None, the function returns None.\n"
}
